title: Pachycladon fastigiatum
taxonomy: Pachycladon fastigiatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. It is native to New Zealand and has been placed in the genus Pachycladon due to its unique characteristics. The classification of Pachycladon fastigiatum is as a flowering plant that belongs to the order Brassicales. Pachycladon fastigiatum is closely related to other species in the Brassicaceae family, such as those in the genera Crambe and Pachycladon. Its classification has been subject to revisions over the years, but it is currently recognized as a distinct species within the family Brassicaceae.

intro: Pachycladon fastigiatum, also known as New Zealand spinach or Raouia, is a succulent plant species native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
